# Striatum: Scaffold A Run

Use this skill to bring a target repo from "no `.striatum/`" to
"a run that's ready to claim work".

## When to invoke

- The operator asked to start a new run.
- The target repo has not yet been registered with the daemon (no
  `.striatum/` directory, or the daemon's registry has no row for it).
- A `workflow.json` is missing or about to be authored.

## Authoritative reference

- `striatum init` ‚Äî Create `.striatum/` scratch in the target repo and register it with the daemon (live state lives in the daemon's PostgreSQL under a `repository_id` scope per D094 / RFC 0043).
- `striatum workflow init` ‚Äî Scaffold a starter `workflow.json` + roles/prompts.
- `striatum workflow validate` ‚Äî Validate a workflow file; non-zero exit on error.
- `striatum run prepare` ‚Äî Snapshot the workflow and prepare a run id.
- `striatum run start` ‚Äî Transition a prepared run to `running`.
- `striatum branch confirm` ‚Äî Confirm or create the run's working branch.

Run `striatum <verb> --help` for full flag sets.

## Common patterns

```bash
striatum --repo /path/to/target init
striatum --repo /path/to/target workflow validate path/to/workflow.json
striatum --repo /path/to/target run prepare --workflow path/to/workflow.json --json
striatum --repo /path/to/target run start --run-id <id> --json
```

`branch.mode: "auto"` is the default; `run prepare` creates the
branch and transitions the run to `ready` in one call. For
`branch.mode: "confirm"` workflows, call `striatum branch confirm
--run-id <id> --create` after `run prepare`.

## Where to look next

- **`striatum-claim-loop`** ‚Äî claim packets, publish artifacts,
  complete jobs.
- **`striatum-recover`** ‚Äî `status`, `why`, `doctor`.

## What not to do

- Do not bypass the daemon (the CLI is the only client; the daemon is the single writer per D094 / RFC 0043); never open Postgres directly, and do not rely on `.striatum/state.sqlite3` ‚Äî on a migrated repo it has been finalized as a read-only `.tombstone` no Striatum verb opens.
- Do not treat marker files, tmux panes, or terminal output as workflow state.
- Do not advance state by printing phrases; advance by calling the CLI verbs in your work packet.
- Do not capture stdout/stderr transcripts; D028 keeps them off by default.
- Do not derive bylines from job titles; use the byline supplied in your work packet.
- Do not parse a supervisor's own output for workflow state; supervisors send DEVNULL.
